title: The drift telemetry punishes a migrated area for growing: count anchor-OWNING concepts, not every file in the directory
action: RED NOW, and the chain is red because of it: `node scripts/okf_migrate.mjs --check okf-profile` reports 'TELEMETRY OUTLIER (F12): okf-profile anchors_per_concept is 3.43, 0.48x the running median 7.13 across 10 pinned areas — outside the [0.5x, 2x] band'. Anchor coverage itself is PERFECT (24 anchors, 24 owned, 0 duplicated, 0 lost) — only the shape metric fired. DIAGNOSIS, already made, verify it before acting: `collectTelemetry()` (scripts/okf_migrate.mjs ~line 1592) computes `concepts = conceptFilesIn(wiringFor(area).dir).length` — EVERY concept file in the area directory — while `anchors` comes only from the PINNED SOURCE. The metric's own stated purpose (see its comment block, ~1556) is detecting decomposition faults of the migrated source: 'too high = a dumping-ground concept'. At migration time every concept in an area owned anchors, so the two counts coincided and the band was calibrated on that coincidence. The moment a migrated area grows a concept of NEW truth — which is the healthy thing for a live area to do — the denominator grows while the numerator cannot, and the area drifts out of band permanently. okf-profile is the first area to hit this because f3-5 migrated it (5 concepts, 24 anchors) and this feature then authored two genuinely new concepts in it (`specs-read-only-fence.md`, `the-instruction-layer-fence.md`), neither of which owns or could own a migrated anchor. FIX: count only concepts that own at least one anchor from the pinned source — i.e. the denominator becomes the number of concepts the coverage walk actually attributes anchors to. This is a CORRECTION OF WHAT IS MEASURED, not a loosened threshold: the band stays [0.5x, 2x], `TELEMETRY_MIN_SAMPLES` stays 3, and no pin's expected_counts change. PROVE IT IS NOT GAMING, and this is the cell's real burden: (a) show the metric still DETECTS what it exists to detect — add a fixture or unit case where a migrated source IS badly decomposed (all anchors dumped in one concept, and the opposite, anchors scattered one-per-concept) and assert the outlier still fires; (b) show every OTHER pinned area's row is UNCHANGED by the new denominator, since at migration time the two counts coincided — quote the before/after table for all 10 pinned areas; (c) state in the code comment why directory-file-count was wrong and why anchor-owning-count is the honest denominator. `concepts_per_100_source_lines` has the SAME defect for the same reason — evaluate it and fix it the same way or state why it differs. Update `scripts/test_okf_pins.mjs` where it asserts telemetry shape.

truths: node scripts/okf_migrate.mjs --check okf-profile PASSES with anchor coverage still 24/24/0/0 and fidelity untouched
before/after telemetry table for ALL 10 pinned areas quoted — every pre-existing area's row unchanged
a negative control proves the metric still bites: a badly-decomposed fixture (all anchors in one concept, and one-anchor-per-concept) still reports an outlier
the band stays [0.5x, 2x] and TELEMETRY_MIN_SAMPLES stays 3 — quote both before and after to prove neither moved
node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0 AND BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0
prohibitions: NEVER widen the outlier band, lower a threshold, or exclude okf-profile from the population — that is the gaming this cell exists to avoid, and it would be a worse defect than the one being fixed
never change any pin's expected_counts or blob_sha
do not delete the telemetry or make it non-failing
if the diagnosis turns out to be wrong, STOP and report — do not force a fix that makes the red go away

trace.outcome: The drift telemetry was punishing a migrated area for growing. Its ratio divided anchors from the PINNED SOURCE by every concept FILE in the area directory, so the moment a migrated area gained a concept of genuinely new truth the denominator grew while the numerator provably could not — okf-profile hit 0.48x and turned the chain red with coverage still perfect at 24/24/0/0. Fixed the DENOMINATOR, not the threshold: it now counts only concepts the coverage walk actually attributes an anchor to. The band stays [0.5x, 2x], min-samples stays 3, no pin moved, and okf-profile was not excluded from the population — all four verified independently, because widening any of them would have made the red vanish and been worse than the bug. The metric is proven to still bite by a negative control in both directions, and the dumping-ground fixture doubles as proof the old denominator was BLIND: 24 anchors dumped in one concept inside a 6-file directory scored 4.00 and sat comfortably in band. The worker also refused the cell's own false must-have — the orchestrator had asserted every other area's row would be unchanged; six move, because five areas already carried authored overview concepts owning no anchor — and reported that instead of trimming the fix to fit. Population is tighter after the change than before.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "RED, and it was the chain's own red at the time the cell was written: `node scripts/okf_migrate.mjs --check okf-profile` -> 'FAIL ... TELEMETRY OUTLIER (F12): okf-profile anchors_per_concept is 3.43, 0.48x the running median 7.13 across 10 pinned areas — outside the [0.5x, 2x] band.' Anchor coverage was PERFECT throughout (24 anchors, 24 owned, 0 duplicated, 0 lost, fidelity 1.000) — only the shape metric fired, which is what pointed at the metric rather than at the area.",
  "diagnosis_confirmed": "The worker verified the mechanism numerically before touching anything: collectTelemetry() took its denominator from conceptFilesIn(dir).length — every concept file in the area directory — while both numerators (anchors, source_lines) come only from the PINNED SOURCE. A migrated area that later grows a concept of genuinely new truth therefore drifts out of band permanently, because the denominator can grow and the numerator provably cannot. okf-profile is the first to hit it: f3-5 migrated it as 5 concepts / 24 anchors, and this feature authored two new concepts in it (specs-read-only-fence.md, the-instruction-layer-fence.md) which own zero anchors and never could. Confirmed: population 10, median 7.125, 24/7 = 3.4286 = 0.481x, just under the 0.5x floor.",
  "the_worker_refused_my_wrong_expectation": "The cell asserted as a must-have that 'every OTHER pinned area's row is UNCHANGED', on the orchestrator's assumption that at migration time the two counts always coincided. THAT ASSUMPTION IS FALSE, and the worker reported it rather than trimming the fix to satisfy it. Five other areas already carry concepts owning no anchor — an authored orientation `overview.md` in advisor-protocol, doctrine-layer, onboarding and workflow-state, plus three post-migration pattern concepts in critical-patterns — so SIX rows move, not one. Every one moves toward honesty (an unmeasurable file stops acting as a denominator) and none lands out of band. This is the correct behavior under the cell's own final prohibition ('if the diagnosis turns out to be wrong, STOP and report — do not force a fix that makes the red go away') applied to the orchestrator's expectation instead of the diagnosis.",
  "before_after_all_pins": "Full 12-row table produced (10 in the ba-nine-section population). Unchanged: decision-memory 9.00, verify-pipeline 7.00, performance-log 7.67, feedback-digest 7.25, hook-runtime 6.75, worktree-parallelism 1.43. Moved: advisor-protocol 6.50->8.67, doctrine-layer 5.57->6.50, onboarding 7.25->8.29, workflow-state 9.33->10.00, critical-patterns 0.94->1.00, okf-profile 3.43->4.80. Resulting medians 7.125->7.4583 (a/c) and 1.1516->1.4026 (c/100L); every area's ratio-to-median now lands in 0.644-1.341 and 0.582-1.782. The population is TIGHTER after the change, not looser — which is the signature of a corrected measurement rather than a loosened one.",
  "negative_control_proves_it_still_bites": "New test section 20b, re-run independently by the orchestrator. Real files walked by the real collectClaims, staged under .bee/tmp/ (not docs/knowledge/, so a concurrently-running suite's bundle invariants never see them) and removed in a finally. Live output: 'dumping-ground 24.00 anchors/concept (file-count would say 4.00) | shredded 1.00 | healthy 4.80', with both bad fixtures reporting TELEMETRY OUTLIER (3.13x and 0.14x) and the healthy one passing — so the fixtures DISCRIMINATE rather than always firing. The dumping-ground case doubles as asserted proof the OLD denominator was blind: 24 anchors in one concept inside a 6-file directory scored 4.00 by file count, comfortably in band, fault invisible.",
  "nothing_loosened": "Verified independently by the orchestrator, not taken on the worker's word: TELEMETRY_MIN_SAMPLES = 3 at line 1609 before and after; the band `ratio > 2 || ratio < 0.5` at line 1687 before and after, and probed from outside in the tests (2.00x passes / 2.01x fails / 0.50x passes / 0.49x fails); `git diff -- scripts/okf_migrate.mjs | grep -c 'expected_counts|blob_sha'` = 0, so no pin moved; okf-profile remains in the population and was not excluded. Coverage and fidelity for okf-profile unchanged at 24/24/0/0 and min/median/max 1.000.",
  "chain": "Re-run INDEPENDENTLY by the orchestrator after the worker reported: `BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs` -> PASS 81 suite(s) exit 0; `node scripts/run_verify.mjs` -> PASS 81 suite(s) exit 0. test_okf_pins now carries 471 assertions, up from 435.",
  "orchestrator_review": "Diff reviewed. One flagged diagnostic investigated and dismissed: `readConceptBodies`'s unused `dir` parameter is PRE-EXISTING at HEAD (same line, same signature) and out of this cell's scope — not introduced here. Two unnamed additions accepted with their stated reasons: `collectClaims` exported (needed to drive the walk end-to-end from a test) and a reported-only `concept_files` field on each row (makes the growth gap between owning concepts and directory files visible rather than silent). `concepts_per_100_source_lines` was correctly identified as carrying the identical defect and fixed from the same variable rather than left as a second latent instance."
}
